commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces OpenClaw's structured real-behavior-proof PR body gate with authored problem/evidence context, updates Barnacle label handling, docs, skills, and focused tests.

PR surface: Tests -216, Docs -81, Other -353. Total -650 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an automation/docs policy PR rather than a runtime bug. Source inspection of PR head and current workflow shows the changed PR context gate path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer rollout and policy approval for PR-body automation, not a mechanical repair.

target.title: improve: simplify PR context and evidence
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The PR template and external-contributor body gate were still tied to a rigid real-behavior-proof form. That made readable PRs harder to write and conflated two separate concerns: author-provided PR context versus ClawSweeper's stronger proof-sufficiency review.

## Why This Change Was Made

Replace the field-level proof form with authored `What Problem This Solves` and `Evidence` sections, and make Barnacle manage only the missing-context label `triage: needs-pr-context`. OpenClaw no longer emits or clears ClawSweeper-owned proof labels such as `proof: supplied`, `proof: sufficient`, or `proof: override`.

Companion ClawSweeper draft: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/pull/319

## User Impact

Authors can write concise PRs that explain intent, design, impact, and the most useful validation evidence. Reviewers and AI can use that context while relying on the diff, tests, CI, and ClawSweeper review as the source of truth for correctness and proof sufficiency.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`
  - 245 tests passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` across all changed OpenClaw files.
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests "<focused tooling command>"`
  - Clean: no accepted or actionable findings.
- ClawSweeper companion validation: `pnpm run check` passed.
target.author: hannesrudolph

commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer review of the intended terminal-session recovery and message-delivery semantics, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff does not touch dependencies, workflows, credentials, permissions, downloads, package metadata, or other concrete security/supply-chain surfaces. (sha=d7cf6d9df3735bdf2e3d7abd8e7f919ba4453181)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates auto-reply session init, reply admission, Gateway agent handling, channel turn logging, and regression tests to recover failed/timeout/killed terminal sessions on visible inbound turns while preserving session identity.

PR surface: Source +177, Tests +588. Total +765 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. with high confidence from reporter live Octo evidence, current-main source tracing, and the PR body's before/after real-runtime proof. I did not run a fresh live channel end-to-end reproduction in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer review of the intended terminal-session recovery and message-delivery semantics, not an automated code repair.
